definition: Represent each perturbation-response example by its biological state or covariate tuple, such as cell line, tissue, treatment condition, donor, or co-culture state. Same covariate context is high-overlap; held-out covariates or covariate tuples define lower-overlap transfer evaluation.
example_usage: python tabular_group_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --group-col covariate

citations.title: PerturBench: Benchmarking Machine Learning Models for Cellular Perturbation Analysis

evidence.summary: The paper defines covariate transfer as predicting perturbation effects in covariates where they were not observed.
The paper creates biologically relevant tasks over perturbation modality, covariates, combinatorial perturbations, and missing data.
The paper describes split parameters for holding out covariates and fractions of perturbations per cell type.

limitations: Binary covariate overlap ignores degrees of biological relatedness between cell states.
Batch, donor, and assay technology may be hidden confounders.
A covariate split does not test unseen perturbation identity unless perturbations are also held out.
quality_gates: Define the exact covariate tuple before computing overlap.
Report whether held-out perturbations were seen in other covariates.
Audit perturbation identity overlap separately from covariate overlap.
